mnemonics: CIA: Capacity, Inventory, Admission Control (Key Levers of Capacity Planning)
keyPoints: Capacity is the maximum amount of work a system can handle.
Planning must account for both current and future demand (growth).
Effective planning balances performance goals with infrastructure costs.
commonMistakes: Focusing only on peak demand and ignoring average utilization.
Procuring hardware based on vague future needs instead of data-driven forecasts.
Neglecting to plan for scalability and elasticity in modern cloud environments.
